text.title: Immersive Design Principles Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the immersive-design-principles methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Locomotion MUST be teleport OR smooth-with-vignette OR a comfort-mode toggle; raw smooth locomotion without vignette is forbidden by default.
rule.rationale: Smooth locomotion without vignette causes nausea in 30-60% of users; vignetting + teleport are the proven safe baseline.

rule.statement: Default session MUST cap at 30 min consumer / 25 min enterprise; longer sessions MUST mandate breaks.
rule.rationale: Fatigue + thermal load on headset compound past 25-30 min.

rule.statement: Spatial audio MUST be used for presence; subtitle + visual-cue fallback MUST exist for HoH users.
rule.rationale: Spatial audio is a presence multiplier; missing fallback excludes HoH users.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list explicit paths for: seated play, single-handed operation, mono-vision, low-vision (high-contrast / large-text)
rule.rationale: Default standing/two-handed/binocular design excludes the a11y tail; explicit paths force the decision.

rule.statement: FOV used for primary interaction MUST be 90-110° (centred); UI placed beyond requires head movement and breaks presence.
rule.rationale: Beyond 110° users miss UI; below 90° feels cramped.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
